item.title: Video Quality Assessment using Space-Time chips
Conditional Wasserstein Generative Adversarial Networks for Image Dehazing
item.description:
Project Supervisor: Professor Sudipta Mukhopadhyay

Achieved state-of-the-art results by training a conditional Wasserstein GAN using the pix2pix model for single image dehazing, with perceptual loss, MSE loss, L1 loss, and texture loss, on the D-Hazy and O-Haze fog datasets, using Pytorch as the programming library.

item.title: FPGA implementation of fog-removal using anisotropic diffusion.
item.description:
Project Supervisor: Professor Sudipta Mukhopadhyay

Achieved 200 fps processing speed in simulations of synthesizable code for Xilinx Zedboard FPGA for high-speed video processing to remove fog from images using anisotropic diffusion. Simulated and verified implementation from scratch on Vivado HLS, synthesized to RTL, and integrated blocks at the system level using Vivado IP integrator.
